comment:
Hi Alex. How's It going: Anyway, I’ve found a couple of pictures that might work well for our project “Celebrating the New Year”
I'd like to discuss them with you.
(Brief description, justifying the choice of the photos for the project)
I think the first photo can be used for our project because it shows a traditional New Year with nine people sitting around a table which is piled up with food and gifts.
In contrast, the second photo works because there is a huge crowd outside with party hats, and confetti falling from the sky and this is a clear representation of how many people enjoy the New Year.
(Differences, justifying the choice of the photos for the project.)
So, together, the pictures illustrate two different ways of celebrating the New Year: staying at home and partying on the street. In the first photo people are sitting at home, cozy and comfortable, whereas in the second photo people are cold and crammed together. Furthermore, in the first photo there are people passing around presents, whereas in the second photo there is nothing but party balloons in people’s hands.
(Advantages and disadvantages (1-2) of the two ways of celebrating the New Year.)
Well, I can see some advantages and disadvantages of the two ways to celebrate the New Year. What I think is an advantage of being at home is that you can visit relatives who you don't get to see very often.
The disadvantage is that you have to spend all day preparing oo for the event.
What’s good about celebrating the New Year on the street is that y can dance and cheer together when the clock strikes midnight.
The downside is that people sometimes push and shove and it’s quite hectic when people are leaving to go home.
(Your opinion on the subject of the project to celebrate the New Year at home and why.)
To tell the truth, I prefer to stay at home for the New Year. Ilike opening presents with my family and seeing my cousins.
(Closing phrase.)
Anyway, I’ll send these photos to you and I want to know if you agree with me. That’s all for now. Talk to you later, bye.
